name: Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán – circular desde Velada por Los Trampalejos
region_intro: Mountain biking around Velada offers a diverse landscape for riders, characterized by open plains, historical features, and views towards the Sierra de Gredos. The region features a mix of terrain, including paths near reservoirs and lagoons, alongside routes that pass through local villages and agricultural areas. Riders can expect varied surfaces, from gravel tracks to more technical sections, providing a range of challenges. Best mountain bike trails around Velada The most popular mountain bike trail is Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán – circular desde Velada por Los Trampalejos, a 46.6 miles (75.0 km) trail that takes 6 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores a Roman bridge and the Navalcán reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Fuente de las Pilas Fountain – View of the Sierra de Gredos loop from Parrillas, a moderate 29.3 miles (47.1 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. Local mountain bikers also love the Palacio Marqueses de Velada – Path to El Marrupejo loop from Velada, a 35.5 miles (57.1 km) trail leading through historical areas and local countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Velada is defined by historical landmarks, open countryside, and views of distant mountain ranges. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Velada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from 9 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Velada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Velada?
A: There are nearly 40 mountain bike trails around Velada, offering a diverse range of options for riders. These include 5 easy routes, 29 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Velada?
A: Yes, Velada offers 5 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Velada?
A: Mountain biking around Velada features a varied landscape, including open plains, paths near reservoirs and lagoons, and routes passing through local villages and agricultural areas. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, from gravel tracks to more technical sections, with views towards the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Velada?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always available, the 5 easy routes in Velada are generally suitable for families with some biking experience. These trails offer a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many routes offer expansive views, particularly towards the Sierra de Gredos mountain range. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10372288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de las Pilas Fountain – View of the Sierra de Gredos loop from Parrillas&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for its scenic vistas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Velada?
A: The mountain bike trails in Velada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from 9 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, historical landmarks, and the open countryside views, especially those of the distant Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Velada?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Velada are designed as loops. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025219026/puente-romano-y-embalse-de-navalcan-circular-desde-velada-por-los-trampalejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán – circular desde Velada por Los Trampalejos&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31471814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio Marqueses de Velada – Path to El Marrupejo loop from Velada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see while mountain biking in Velada?
A: The region is rich in historical features. Routes often pass by significant sites such as the Roman bridge on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025219026/puente-romano-y-embalse-de-navalcan-circular-desde-velada-por-los-trampalejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán&lt;/a&gt; trail, or through areas with historical significance like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31471814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio Marqueses de Velada – Path to El Marrupejo loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or bodies of water along the trails?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate natural water features. You can explore areas near the Navalcán reservoir, as seen on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025219026/puente-romano-y-embalse-de-navalcan-circular-desde-velada-por-los-trampalejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán&lt;/a&gt; trail, or discover lagoons on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Descent to the Gamonal Circuit – Lagoons loop from Segurilla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some nearby attractions to explore after a mountain bike ride?
A: After your ride, you can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Count’s Palace of Oropesa and Oropesa Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For more natural exploration, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6116045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3983842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Águila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any advanced mountain biking options in Velada?
A: Yes, Velada offers 5 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These trails typically feature longer distances, significant elevation changes, and more technical sections. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025219026/puente-romano-y-embalse-de-navalcan-circular-desde-velada-por-los-trampalejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán – circular desde Velada por Los Trampalejos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 75 km.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Velada?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on difficulty and distance. Easy routes might take a couple of hours, while moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38177516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Sapo loop from Oropesa&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under 3 hours. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025219026/puente-romano-y-embalse-de-navalcan-circular-desde-velada-por-los-trampalejos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente romano y embalse de Navalcán&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 6 hours.
tips: The area surrounding the town of Montesclaros is known for its marble deposits that have been exploited for its extraction. Today, many of them are no longer in use and can be visited by following the circular marked itinerary that crosses a beautiful landscape with lots of greenery and a very picturesque setting.
In the area of Los Trampalejos is this panoramic point with beautiful views of the Sierra de Gredos Natural Park in the neighboring province of Ávila.

Saving the Guadyerbas riverbed, the Romans built this particular bridge a little over 2000 years ago. The curious thing is its striking arch, longer than normal and with a very slender and thin board.

waypoints.name: View of the Sierra de Gredos from Fuente de las Pilas
